target.name: OpenAI
target.founder: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $9.7B - $183.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $183B

private.name: Anysphere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $29.3B

private.name: Neuralink
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $9.7B

data.narrative:
OpenAI is a late-stage private AI & Software company founded by Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, and Ilya Sutskever. The valuation universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir) and eight private peers in the AI and software sector, including Anthropic, Anysphere, Neuralink, and xAI.

The peer-median method was the applicable triangulation approach. A cohort of three same-sector, same-stage private companies—Anthropic ($183.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), and Neuralink ($9.7B)—yielded a median valuation of $29.3B. This method captures material heterogeneity within the frontier AI space: the peer range spans from $9.7B to $183.0B, reflecting significant variation in investor conviction and business model maturity across the subset.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $24.9B to $33.7B, centered on a $29.3B median. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of a three-company peer set given dispersion in the comp universe, the limited applicability of public comps to late-stage private AI ventures with heterogeneous business models, and the absence of revenue or unit-economics data to anchor relative valuation metrics. The current stated valuation of $300B+ materially exceeds the peer-derived range, signaling either pricing power not yet reflected in comparable transactions or valuation compression risk.
